preferred_translation: mental composure that comes forth from emptiness
alternative_translations: mental composure that comes forth from voidness
definition: Mental composure governed by, or coming forth from, the emptiness side of the emptiness / signless / wishless family.
notes: Keep this compound tied to suññatā as emptiness rather than treating it as interchangeable with signless or wishless compounds. This repository now prefers a fuller English rendering here so the compound does not read like a frozen technical label detached from practice. The compound inherits the headword's anti-nihilist and anti-mystifying policy, and voidness remains a controlled alternate when the Buddhadasa-line idiom itself is doing explanatory work.
